<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling is expected — static assets have no import graph edges and form a natural isolated cluster.
- The HTML file is the runtime host document for the viewer SPA; it must be kept in sync with the viewer bundle's output filenames and script references.
- Logo assets (darkmode_logo.png, lightmode_logo.png) being separate files enables CSS-based theme switching without JavaScript — a clean static approach.
- Static asset zones (images, HTML) trivially achieve cohesion 1.0 and coupling 0.0 by virtue of having no imports — these metrics should be interpreted structurally rather than as a quality signal.
- The HTML shell must reference the correct bundled JS/CSS output paths; any rename of viewer bundle output files requires a corresponding update to index.html — consider automating this via the build pipeline (e.g. html-webpack-plugin or equivalent).
- Two logo variants suggest dark mode support is implemented at the asset level; verify the viewer's theme-switching logic correctly toggles between these assets to avoid a flash of wrong-theme logo on load.
- Zero coupling to landing-page confirms the two HTML zones are correctly separated; the shell's only relationship is structural (it hosts the viewer bundle) rather than import-based, which is the correct pattern for an app shell.
- viewer-html-shell has no import edges to any other zone, relying entirely on the build pipeline to inject the viewer bundle — this is the correct pattern but means build-config drift (e.g. output filename changes) cannot be caught by import-graph analysis alone; a dedicated build integration test is warranted.
- Zone "logo" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/logo/ and moving related files there

</insights>
